title: asclepias fragrans
intro: Asclepias fragrans, also known as Fragrant Milkweed, is a perennial plant species in the Asclepiadaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate milk production in lactating women and its use in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Asclepias fragrans include its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce pain. The plant's roots and leaves can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
